dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8099 IH: Plug Offshore Wells Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Dexter (for herself, Ms. Bonamici, Mr. Huffman, Mr. Min, Mr. Kennedy of New York, Ms. Brownley, Ms. Norton, Mr. Mullin, and Mr. Levin) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of the Interior to annually submit to Congress, and make publicly available on a website, a report on decommissioning offshore oil and gas wells, platforms, and pipelines, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Plug Offshore Wells Act.

section.header: Annual report on decommissioning offshore oil and gas wells, platforms, and pipelines

paragraph.text: The term decommissioning has the meaning given the term in section 250.1700 of title 30, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation).
The term Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior.

subsection.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act, and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress and make publicly available on the website of the Department of the Interior a report that describes, with respect to the preceding calendar year—

paragraph.text: the number of applications for decommissioning an offshore oil and gas well, platform, or pipeline that were required to be submitted pursuant to subpart Q of part 250 of title 30, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations);
the number of applications described in paragraph (1) that were received by the Secretary;

the number of offshore oil and gas wells, platforms, and pipelines for which decommissioning did not occur by the date required pursuant to subpart Q of part 250 of title 30, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations);
the number of offshore oil and gas wells and platforms approved for decommissioning in place pursuant to section 250.1750 of title 30, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation);
the length of any offshore oil and gas pipelines that—

subparagraph.text: were decommissioned in place pursuant to section 250.1750 of title 30, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation); and

clause.text: section 250.1752 of title 30, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation); and
section 250.1754 of title 30, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation); and

paragraph.text: the status of enforcement actions, including notices of incidents of noncompliance, orders, citations, civil penalties, and disqualifications from future offshore operations, by the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement with respect to decommissioning offshore oil and gas wells, platforms, and pipelines.
